title: Deploy loopctl to Fly.io with managed PostgreSQL

story.i_want_to: deploy loopctl to Fly.io with a managed PostgreSQL database and automated CI/CD
story.so_that: the service is publicly accessible at loopctl.com with production-grade reliability, automatic deployments on merge to master, and no dependency on the self-hosted beelink server

rationale: loopctl is currently self-hosted on a beelink machine behind a residential network. To offer it as a public service, it needs to run on cloud infrastructure with proper SSL, managed database, health monitoring, and automated deploys. Fly.io is the proven platform — both home_care_billing and reportex already run there successfully with the same Phoenix/Elixir stack.

acceptance_criteria.description: fly.toml updated to production-ready: app 'loopctl', primary_region 'lax', PHX_HOST 'loopctl.com', PORT '8080', internal_port 8080, min_machines_running 1, auto_stop_machines 'suspend' (NOT true — true stops all machines contradicting min_machines_running), health check GET /health every 10s.

Dockerfile updated: COPY assets assets added to builder stage, RUN mix assets.deploy added after mix compile (esbuild and tailwind are standalone binaries — Node.js is NOT needed), EXPOSE 8080, ENV PORT 8080. tini kept as init process (Fly does NOT provide an init — the BEAM is PID 1 without it).

Fly Managed PostgreSQL provisioned. RLS roles created via one-time manual setup through 'fly postgres connect': deploy/fly-db-setup.sh contains the complete SQL: CREATE ROLE loopctl_app LOGIN PASSWORD '...', CREATE ROLE loopctl_admin LOGIN BYPASSRLS PASSWORD '...', GRANT ALL ON ALL TABLES/SEQUENCES, CREATE FUNCTION current_tenant_id(). Script content is fully specified in this story's technical notes.

DATABASE_URL uses direct Postgres connection (port 5433, bypassing PgBouncer) to ensure SET LOCAL works for RLS. Format: postgres://loopctl_app:PASS@loopctl-db.flycast:5433/loopctl?sslmode=disable. ADMIN_DATABASE_URL uses loopctl_admin role. Both set as Fly secrets.

Runtime config (config/runtime.exs) adds DB connection tuning inside the existing config_env() == :prod block: connect_timeout 15_000, queue_target 5_000, queue_interval 10_000. These apply to ALL prod deployments (not Fly-only). IPv6 enabled via ECTO_IPV6.
All required secrets set via 'fly secrets set': DATABASE_URL, ADMIN_DATABASE_URL, SECRET_KEY_BASE, CLOAK_KEY. No secrets in fly.toml or Dockerfile.

Release infrastructure created: lib/loopctl/release.ex module with migrate/0 function that calls Ecto.Migrator.run(Loopctl.AdminRepo, ...) (must use AdminRepo for BYPASSRLS to create RLS policies). rel/overlays/bin/migrate and rel/overlays/bin/server scripts. fly.toml [deploy] release_command = '/app/bin/migrate'.

DNS configured: loopctl.com uses A record pointing to Fly.io anycast IPs (from 'fly ips list'), NOT a CNAME (CNAME on apex domain violates RFC 1912). Fly.io issues SSL certificate via 'fly certs add loopctl.com'. force_https true in fly.toml.

GitHub Actions CI/CD: beelink deploy job preserved for backward compatibility. New Fly.io deploy job added as a separate parallel job using superfly/flyctl-actions and 'flyctl deploy --remote-only'. Both deploy targets run after quality gates pass. FLY_API_TOKEN stored as GitHub secret.

fly.toml HTTP options: h2_backend = false (defensive — applicable at Fly proxy level regardless of Bandit adapter), session affinity not configured (single machine with min_machines_running = 1 makes affinity irrelevant; add when scaling to multiple machines).

After deployment: GET https://loopctl.com returns the landing page, GET https://loopctl.com/health returns JSON {status: ok}, GET https://loopctl.com/api/v1/ returns API discovery JSON. All API endpoints functional with RLS enforced.
Beelink docker-compose deployment continues to work unchanged. PORT (default 4000) is the only environment difference. No code changes break the existing setup.

test_cases.name: Health check endpoint returns ok on Fly.io
expected_results: Returns 200 with JSON {status: ok, database: connected}

test_cases.name: API routes accessible and RLS enforced
preconditions: Two tenants with API keys provisioned
steps: Create project under tenant A, list as tenant B
expected_results: Tenant B cannot see tenant A's project
SET LOCAL persists within transaction (not broken by PgBouncer)

test_cases.name: Landing page served on Fly.io
preconditions: App deployed with US-18.1 landing page

test_cases.name: HTTPS enforced and certificate valid
expected_results: HTTPS returns 200 with valid TLS certificate

test_cases.name: Migrations run on deploy
steps: flyctl deploy, check release command logs
expected_results: RLS policies exist on tenant-scoped tables

test_cases.name: CI/CD pipeline deploys on master push
steps: Push commit to master, monitor workflow
expected_results: All quality gate jobs pass (lint, test, security, dialyzer — run in parallel)
Fly.io deploy job runs after gates pass

test_cases.name: Release module uses AdminRepo for migrations
steps: Inspect Loopctl.Release.migrate/0 source to verify it calls AdminRepo

technical_notes: Reference deployments: home_care_billing and reportex (fly.toml, Dockerfile, .github/workflows/ci.yml)

CRITICAL: Node.js is NOT needed. Esbuild and tailwind Hex packages download standalone platform-specific binaries. The Dockerfile needs COPY assets assets + RUN mix assets.deploy — not apt-get install nodejs
CRITICAL: PgBouncer breaks SET LOCAL for RLS. Use DIRECT connections on port 5433 (not 5432). Format: postgres://role:pass@db.flycast:5433/loopctl?sslmode=disable
CRITICAL: auto_stop_machines must be 'suspend' not true. true stops machines and contradicts min_machines_running = 1

CRITICAL: Create lib/loopctl/release.ex with migrate/0 calling Ecto.Migrator.run(Loopctl.AdminRepo, ...). AdminRepo is required for BYPASSRLS privilege when creating RLS policies. Also create rel/overlays/bin/migrate and rel/overlays/bin/server scripts

fly-db-setup.sh must contain: CREATE ROLE loopctl_app WITH LOGIN PASSWORD 'xxx'; CREATE ROLE loopctl_admin WITH LOGIN BYPASSRLS PASSWORD 'xxx'; GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE loopctl TO loopctl_app, loopctl_admin; GRANT ALL ON ALL TABLES IN SCHEMA public TO loopctl_app, loopctl_admin; GRANT ALL ON ALL SEQUENCES IN SCHEMA public TO loopctl_app, loopctl_admin; ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA public GRANT ALL ON TABLES TO loopctl_app, loopctl_admin; ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES IN SCHEMA public GRANT ALL ON SEQUENCES TO loopctl_app, loopctl_admin; CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION current_tenant_id() RETURNS uuid AS $$ SELECT nullif(current_setting('app.current_tenant_id', true), '')::uuid $$ LANGUAGE sql STABLE
DNS: apex domain (loopctl.com) requires A record pointing to Fly anycast IPs (fly ips list), NOT CNAME. CNAME at zone apex violates RFC 1912. Use CNAME only for www.loopctl.com
CI: keep beelink deploy job. Add Fly.io deploy as a NEW parallel job (both targets get deployed on master push). Use superfly/flyctl-actions/setup-flyctl@master
Session affinity: not needed with min_machines_running = 1 (single target). Add when scaling to multiple machines
h2_backend = false: set defensively at Fly proxy level. Works regardless of Bandit vs Cowboy
DB tuning: add connect_timeout, queue_target, queue_interval to the EXISTING prod Repo config block in runtime.exs. Applies to all prod deployments (beelink + Fly). Not Fly-specific
Oban: uses Loopctl.Repo which inherits the direct-connection DATABASE_URL. Webhook delivery worker has outbound HTTP access on Fly by default
